description: Brief that Lumen.fm solves exactly. Must surface Lumen with score >= 80 in Phase A.

briefContent.title: API mock server with custom scripting and team sharing

problem.trigger: Our frontend team is blocked every time the backend API is not ready
problem.currentWorkaround: Using Lumen for basic mocking but missing custom response scripting

desiredOutcome.definitionOfGoodEnough: Frontend team can develop against realistic API responses without waiting for backend

description: Vague creative brief, no app in pool matches. Must go to builder matching with >= 3 builders.

briefContent.title: AI-powered brand identity system for indie music labels

problem.trigger: After signing 3 new artists in Q3, we can't maintain consistent brand across all their releases
problem.currentWorkaround: Manual Figma templates but each release still looks inconsistent

desiredOutcome.definitionOfGoodEnough: Each artist has a living brand system that auto-adapts to new releases while staying consistent
mustHaves: AI-generated visual variations from brand guidelines
outOfScope: General graphic design tool (we have Figma)

description: Exploratory budget brief. App pool contains only irrelevant apps so Phase A finds nothing strong. Builders with rate_band GT_10K must be filtered out by the rate-band check.

briefContent.title: Simple CSV import tool for non-technical users

problem.trigger: New intern can't figure out how to get data into our system
problem.currentWorkaround: Asking engineers to do manual imports

desiredOutcome.definitionOfGoodEnough: Non-technical users can import CSV files without engineering help

description: Brief with geography LATAM only. Builders outside LATAM must be excluded.

briefContent.title: Local delivery route optimizer for LATAM last-mile logistics

problem.trigger: Our fleet of 20 drivers is covering routes manually, leading to 40% extra distance driven
problem.affected: Fleet managers and 20 drivers in Brazil, Mexico, Colombia
problem.currentWorkaround: WhatsApp group + driver intuition for routing

desiredOutcome.definitionOfGoodEnough: Automated route optimization that reduces average distance by 25% within first month
mustHaves: Driver mobile app in Spanish and Portuguese

description: SEMANTIC CASE — brief uses paraphrase vocabulary instead of the app's exact keywords. The seeker says 'stand-in server' and 'canned responses' rather than 'mock server' and 'scripted replies'. The intent is identical to match_001 (Lumen), but keyword overlap is low. In the hermetic eval Lumen is still in the synthetic pool, so this case exercises only whether the re-ranker recognises paraphrased intent — it does NOT validate vector retrieval against a live corpus (that would require a real DB + Voyage). Coverage for the vector arm's contribution to ordering lives in lib/wanted/matching/semantic-retriever.test.ts and rrf.test.ts.

briefContent.title: Stand-in server that returns canned responses while backend is unavailable

problem.trigger: Client-side engineers keep sitting idle whenever the server-side team is still building endpoints
problem.currentWorkaround: Hard-coding JSON fixtures directly in the frontend — messy and hard to clean up

desiredOutcome.definitionOfGoodEnough: Client engineers can continue building against realistic responses without any dependency on the backend being ready
mustHaves: Intercept HTTP calls and return configurable canned responses
Share response configurations across the team
Load an endpoint contract file to seed the stand-in server

description: Brief mentions seeker already uses Lumen. Lumen must NOT appear as an app match.

briefContent.title: Postman alternative with better team collaboration and version control

problem.trigger: After team grew to 10 engineers, our shared Postman workspace became a mess of outdated collections
problem.currentWorkaround: Using Lumen for mocking and Postman for API testing, but they don't integrate

desiredOutcome.definitionOfGoodEnough: All engineers working from a single source of truth for API collections with no stale data
mustHaves: Git-based version control for API collections
outOfScope: API gateway (we have AWS API Gateway)
